note: Typical climate norms for the Medora / Theodore Roosevelt National Park area (western North Dakota). hi/lo in °F, precip in inches. Approximate long-term averages for planning — not a forecast. months indexed Jan..Dec.
source: Regional climate normals, western North Dakota Badlands

months.note: Warm; the wettest month, pop-up storms
Hot and mostly dry; occasional storms
Mild days, cold nights; fall color late

summer.label: Summer
pack: Lightweight, breathable layers
Wide-brim hat and sunglasses
High-SPF sunscreen — there's little shade in the Badlands
A refillable water bottle; it's dry country
Sturdy closed-toe walking shoes
A light rain shell for afternoon storms
Insect repellent (ticks and mosquitoes, especially early summer)
A warm layer for cool evenings and the outdoor Medora Musical
prepare: Hot afternoons (often 85–95°F) with intense sun and wind — hydrate and reapply sunscreen
Thunderstorms can build fast; check radar and get off exposed buttes if lightning or hail threatens
Nights cool off quickly — the Musical is outdoors, so bring a layer
Give bison and wildlife a wide berth, and expect spotty cell service in the park

spring.label: Spring
pack: Layers you can add and shed through the day
A warm insulated jacket and a rain shell
Gloves and a hat for chilly mornings
Waterproof shoes for muddy trails
Sunscreen and sunglasses
prepare: Big day-to-night temperature swings and strong wind
Trails get slick — Badlands clay turns to gumbo when wet
Some seasonal attractions and shows don't open until late May (Memorial Day)

fall.label: Fall
pack: Layers for warm days and cold nights
A warm jacket and gloves for mornings
A rain shell for passing showers
Sturdy walking shoes
Sunscreen and sunglasses
prepare: Comfortable days but cold nights — first frosts arrive by late September
Wind is common; fall color peaks late September into early October
Seasonal shows and some attractions wind down after mid-September

winter.label: Winter
pack: A heavy insulated coat, warm hat, and gloves
Insulated, waterproof boots
Thermal base layers and warm mid-layers
Traction cleats for icy paths
Sunglasses for snow glare
prepare: Cold, with wind chills that can drop well below zero and blowing snow
Roads may be snow-packed or icy — check ND 511 before driving and carry a winter emergency kit
Short daylight; plan outdoor time around midday
Many Medora shops, restaurants and the Musical are closed off-season — the Library and the park stay open year-round
